hook: Chart walls and CD singles at every mall, gone by 2006
fate: Most U.S. stores closed or were converted to FYE after The Musicland Group filed for Chapter 11 in 2006.
summary: Mall music retailer known for its wide CD and cassette selection, chart walls, and listening stations. Most stores disappeared after Musicland's 2006 bankruptcy.
overview_md:
**Sam Goody** was a music store chain that became a go-to spot for music fans at malls across the country. It was founded in New York City in 1951 and grew into one of the most familiar names in music retail. The stores were known for their chart walls, listening stations, and a wide selection of CDs, cassettes, and singles.

Through the 1980s and 1990s, **Sam Goody** expanded to hundreds of mall locations. Staff knew their music, new releases got their own section each Tuesday, and the selection went well beyond the hits into back-catalog albums and imports.

Things got harder in the early 2000s. Big-box retailers like Walmart and Target started selling CDs at lower prices. Then came file sharing and paid downloads, which cut into music sales industry-wide. Filling large stores with physical inventory became expensive, and profits dropped.

**Sam Goody** was owned by The Musicland Group, which could not hold up under these pressures. After years of store closures and losses, Musicland filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2006. Many **Sam Goody** locations shut down, while others were taken over by Trans World Entertainment and rebranded as FYE. The name was gone from most malls within a few years.

For a lot of people who grew up in the 1980s and 1990s, **Sam Goody** brings back the memory of flipping through CDs, grabbing a single on the way out, or discovering a new band on a Tuesday afternoon.

timeline.text: Sam Goody opens in New York City
Sam Goody becomes part of The Musicland Group
Best Buy acquires The Musicland Group
Best Buy sells Musicland back as a standalone company
Musicland files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy; most Sam Goody stores close or convert to FYE

seo.meta_description: Sam Goody was America's go-to mall music store from 1951 to 2006, known for chart walls and CD singles. Most stores closed after Musicland Group's 2006 bankruptcy.
